data.title: HD Korea Shipbuilding & Offshore Engineering Target Price Raised to KRW347,000 From KRW290,000 by Kiwoom Securities >009540.SE
Atomo Diagnostics Appoints CEO John Kelly as Interim Chair, Shares Up 9%
data.description:
Atomo Diagnostics has appointed John Kelly, its current chief executive officer, as interim chair of the board, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

The company said there will be no change to Kelly's remuneration.

The company's shares rose 9% in recent Friday trade.

data.title: Alara Dispatches 17th Copper-Gold Concentrate Shipment From Oman Mine; Shares Fall 4%
data.description:
Alara Resources dispatched the 17th shipment of copper-gold concentrate from its Al Wash-hi-Majaza mine in Oman on Thursday, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

The copper-concentrate plant produced 1,277 wet metric tonnes of copper-gold concentrate, containing around 223.6 metric tonnes of copper and 2.9 kilograms of gold in the latest shipping cycle, the filing said.

The mine is operated by the firm's joint venture Al Hadeetha Resources, in which it holds a 51% stake, per the filing.

Alara Resources' shares fell 4% in recent Friday trade.

data.title: The Blockchain Group Closes EUR21 Million Purchase of Bitcoins
data.description:
The Blockchain Group said Thursday it completed the acquisition of 227 Bitcoins for a combined consideration of 21.2 million euros.

The transaction increased the Bitcoin treasury company's Bitcoin holding to 847, reflecting a valuation of 71.7 million euros.

data.title: Saudi Regulator Approves Mulkia Investment's Capital Increase
data.description:
Saudi Arabia's Capital Market Authority approved Mulkia Investment Co.'s (SASE:9585) proposed capital increase by issuing one bonus share for every five existing shares.

The financial and investment services company's capital will increase to 78 million Saudi riyals from 65 million riyals through the transfer of 13 million riyals from the company's retained earnings, according to a Thursday notice.

The regulator added that the company is expected to hold its extraordinary general assembly within the next six months and satisfy regulatory and legal requirements.

data.title: COSCO Shipping International (Singapore) Appoints CFO
data.description:
COSCO Shipping International (Singapore) appointed Zhang Jie as the company's chief financial officer and chief risk officer, effective May 22, according to a same-day filing with the Singapore Exchange.

Jie will oversee the group's financial and risk management through both roles.

Most recently, Jie served as general manager, corporate finance division of COSCO SHIPPING (North America).

data.title: India's Gujarat State Petronet falls on quarterly profit decline
data.description:
** Shares of Gujarat State Petronet fall 1.9% to 347 rupees

** co posts a 46.4% Y/Y decline in Q4 consolidated profit to 3.55 billion rupees ($41.34 mln)

** Rev falls 4.57%, while expenses rise 4.98%

** Higher volumes, lower operating expenses to partly offset tariff cut impact; Nuvama Research says

** Stock rated "Hold" on avg; median PT is 360 rupees, per data compiled by LSEG

** Stock down 6% YTD

($1 = 85.8650 Indian rupees)

data.title: India Shares Rise But Head for Losses Weekly
data.description:
India's equities rose 614 points, or 0.8%, to 81,541 in early trading on Friday, partly recovering from the previous day's decline amid gains across almost all sectors, led by tech stocks.

The BSE Sensex rebounded from its lowest level in nearly two weeks, touched the day before, while the Nifty 50 climbed 0.8% to above 24,800.

Optimism about the US-China trade negotiations lifted sentiment after Washington and Beijing agreed to maintain open communication.

However, concerns over foreign fund outflows amid rising US Treasury yields and a weakening US fiscal outlook capped the gains.

For the week, the index is heading for a 1.0% drop, after surging the previous week, weighed down by concerns over mounting debt and Moody's downgrade of the US credit rating last week.

Among the top gainers were Infosys (1.9%), Tech Mahindra (1.8%), ITC (1.8%), HCL Technologies (1.5%), and UltraTech Cement (1.4%).

data.title: India's Waaree Energies falls on likely hit to  US order book from budget bill
data.description:
** Shares of Waaree Energies fall 9.5% to 2,711.70 rupees

** US House of Representatives passed a bill that accelerates the phasing out of investment and production tax credits for clean energy projects

** Jefferies sees risk to 57% of WAAN's order book that comes from the US due to the bill

** WAAN's manufacturing plant in US should be eligible for production tax credits till 2031 - Jefferies note

** Jefferies has "underperform" rating on WAAN with PT at 2,100 rupees, ~30% below the prev close

** WAAN down ~5% YTD

data.title: Romania sells 64 mln euro of 3-mo T-bills, below target
data.description:
BUCHAREST (Romania), May 23 (SeeNews) - Romania's finance ministry sold on Thursday 323.1 million lei ($72.2 million/63.9 million euro) worth of discount Treasury bills maturing on August 27, 2025, below target, central bank data showed.

The average accepted yield came in at 7.33%, with demand totalling 696.1 million lei, the central bank said in an auction data release.

data.title: Lykos Appoints Non-Executive Chair, Interim CEO
data.description:
Lykos Metals said current Non-Executive Director, David Wheeler has been appointed as non-executive chair, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

Wheeler will succeed outgoing Non-Executive Chairman Anna Sloboda, the filing said.

Current Executive Director and Non-Executive Director of GTI Energy and Regenerate Resources , Petar Tomasevic, has been appointed as interim chief executive officer, effective Friday, the filing added.

Tomasevic will earn a remuneration of AU$200,000 per annum, the company said.

Shares of GTI Energy rose 14% in recent Friday trade.

data.title: India stocks, rupee, bonds, swaps, call at 10:00 a.m. IST
data.description:
STOCKS:

The benchmark BSE Sensex was up 0.7% to 81515, while the broader NSE Nifty 50 rose 0.8% to 24797, aided by gains in shares of information technology and consumer goods' companies.

RUPEE:

The Indian rupee rose 0.2% versus the U.S. dollar to 85.8375, tracking a rise in Asian peers as the dollar remained on the defensive due to worries about U.S. fiscal health.

GOVERNMENT BONDS:

The benchmark 10-year bond (IN063335G=CC) was quoted at 100.87 rupees, with the yield down 2 bps at 6.2107% as investors stepped in after a late-session sell-off the previous day, though attention remained on the upcoming weekly debt auction.

data.title: Chinese Drugmaker Hengrui Shares Jump in Strong Hong Kong Debut
data.description:
By Tracy Qu

Shares of Chinese drugmaker Jiangsu Hengrui Pharmaceuticals soared on their Hong Kong debut, highlighting a surge in IPO activity as more companies seek to tap the city's capital markets.

Hengrui's stock jumped 31% Friday morning to 57.65 Hong Kong dollars, while the benchmark Hang Seng Index edged up 0.6%.

The Chinese drugmaker raised HK$9.74 billion, equivalent to US$1.25 billion, in net proceeds, making it one of Hong Kong's largest IPOs so far this year. The stock was priced at HK$44.05 per share, the upper end of its guidance range.

Meanwhile, Hengrui's Shanghai-listed shares were last down 0.1%.

Another pharmaceutical company also saw a strong debut in Hong Kong on Friday. Singapore-based Mirxes, which focuses on cancer treatment, raised HK$880.5 million in net proceeds. Its shares surged 28% in morning trading.

Hong Kong's IPO market has gained momentum in recent months, largely driven by Chinese firms seeking second listings in the financial hub. Increased participation from A-share companies has supported a rebound in IPO activity this year, the Hong Kong Stock Exchange said last month.

Funds raised from 17 new listings in the first quarter nearly quadrupled from a year earlier to HK$18.7 billion, according to HKEX.

Friday's listings follow a blockbuster offering by Chinese battery giant Contemporary Amperex Technology, a key Tesla supplier. The company, also listed on Shenzhen's ChiNext board, raised over US$4.6 billion--the largest equity offering globally so far this year.

Write to Tracy Qu at tracy.qu@wsj.com

data.title: Honda Expands Production Capacity by Adding New Production Line at Fourth Motorcycle Plant in India
data.description:
Ahmedabad, India, May 23, 2025 - (JCN Newswire) - Honda Motorcycle & Scooter India Pvt. Ltd. (HMSI), Honda's motorcycle production and sales subsidiary in India, will build a fourth production line at its fourth plant (Vithalapur, Ahmedabad district, Gujarat). Planned to begin operation in 2027, the new line will have an annual production capacity of 650,000 units, bringing the total capacity of the fourth plant to 2.61 million units, making it Honda's largest assembly plant for Honda motorcycles in the world.

HMSI currently has four production plants in India with a total annual production capacity of 6.14 million units. In addition, the cumulative production volume reached 70 million units in April of this year, after 25 years since start of production in 2001.The fourth plant started operation in February 2016 with an annual production capacity of 600,000 units, and in June of the same year, the company expanded its capacity to 1.2 million units with the start of its second line. Furthermore, the third line started operation in January 2024, taking annual production capacity to 1.96 million units.Honda will be investing approximately 9.2 billion rupees (1 rupee = 1.75 yen, and approximately 16.1 billion Japanese yen) to construct a fourth line capable of producing 650,000 units of 125cc class motorcycles per year on the premises of the fourth plant.This will bring 1800 new jobs and increase the total production capacity of the fourth plant to 2.61 million units, making it one of Honda's largest assembly plants for motorcycles in the world.In addition to the fourth production line at the fourth plant, HMSI's total annual production capacity is expected to increase from the current 6.14 million units to approximately 7 million units in 2027 through further expansion of production capacity planned for other plants in India.

Tsutsumu Otani, HMSI President & CEO

"Honda has long been investing and expanding its production capacity in India, the world's largest motorcycle market, to bring joy to its customers. 25 years on, with much support, HMSI has reached the milestone of 70 million units of cumulative production. With the additional investment in our fourth plant, Honda will continue to deliver attractive products and services to its customers around the world who have higher expectations and trust in the company, and further solidify its Indian motorcycle business."

Source: Honda Motor Co, Ltd

Copyright 2025 JCN Newswire . All rights reserved.

data.title: Australia, New Zealand dollars gain but face stiff chart resistance
data.description:
The Australian and New Zealand dollars gained on Friday as an overnight bounce in the U.S. currency faded, but they still face some heavy resistance levels that have kept them mostly range-bound.

The Aussie rose 0.4% to $0.6437, recouping all its overnight losses, and is set to end the week up 0.5%. It faces some stiff resistance at $0.6450 and $0.6500/6515, while support is solid at $0.6360.

The kiwi inched up 0.3% to $0.5917, having lost 0.7% overnight and bringing the weekly rise to 0.6%. It stayed corralled in a range of $0.5847 to $0.6029 that has held for the past month.

The U.S. dollar bounced overnight partly due to the passage of President Donald Trump's bill for huge tax and spending cuts by the House of Representatives, but the lift proved short-lived on Friday as investors stayed bearish about the fate of the dollar.

More analysts are turning bullish on the Australian dollar.

"The worst of the external backdrop for AUD looks to be passing," said Lachlan Dynan, a macro strategist at Deutsche Bank.

"China's credit impulse is around its highest in four years and the fiscal impulse has lifted. Combined with the US-China climbdown on tariffs, the outlook for AU export demand looks better than many had feared," he added.

"The more dovish RBA shouldn't hamper AUD."

On Thursday, the Reserve Bank of Australia's deputy governor, Andrew Hauser, said Australian exporters were upbeat on the business outlook in China, despite the trade war.

Governor Michele Bullock will visit China next week to meet her counterparts.

The coming week is set to be busy for the two Antipodeans, with the Reserve Bank of New Zealand's policy meet on Wednesday, at which it is widely expected to cut its Official Cash Rate (OCR) to 3.25%, though uncertainty remains about its next moves.

Australia will see the release of data on monthly CPI on Wednesday and retail sales on Friday, which have gained more importance after its central bank opened the door to further policy easing.

A rate cut in July is now priced at a 60% probability, with a total easing of 65 basis points expected by year-end. (0#AUDIRPR)

Local bond markets, which have cheered the RBA's pivot, are set for bumper gains this week. Australian three-year government bond yields (AU3YT=RR) fell 13 bps in the week to 3.499%, their biggest weekly drop since early April.

The company has invited bids from bankers and investors on Monday, they said.

L&T Finance did not reply to a Reuters email seeking comment.

data.title: Uzma Unit, Infrasel to Collaborate on Slope Monitoring Using Satellite
data.description:
Uzma unit Geospatial AI signed a memorandum of understanding with Infrasel to explore collaboration on slope monitoring and managing infrastructure risks in Selangor, Malaysia, using satellite technology.

The arrangement will be valid for one year, from May 22, 2025 to May 21, 2026, with an option to extend, according to a Thursday Malaysian bourse filing.

data.title: Turkey - Factors To Watch on May 23
data.description:
Here are news, reports and events that may affect Turkish financial markets on Friday.

The lira was quoted at 39.0180 against the U.S. dollar at 0440 GMT, compared with a close of 39.0210 on Thursday.

The main BIST 100 share index closed 0.81% higher at 9,475.21 points on Thursday.

GLOBAL MARKETS

Asian shares made some tentative gains on Friday as beaten-down Treasuries found buyers after U.S. President Donald Trump's tax bill narrowly passed the lower house, although debt worries still lingered.

ERDOGAN PROGRAMME

President Tayyip Erdogan will attend the International Family Forum in Istanbul. (1130 GMT)

VICE PRESIDENT

Vice President Cevdet Yilmaz will attend an economic council organised by the Union of Chambers and Commodity Exchanges of Turkey in Ankara. (1100 GMT)

ENERGY MINISTER

Energy Minister Alparslan Bayraktar will visit the local governor's and mayor's offices, as well as the ruling AK Party office, in the eastern province of Erzincan. He will later meet students at a university there. (0750 GMT)

TRANSPORT MINISTER

Transport and Infrastructure Minister Abdulkadir Uraloglu will hold talks with his Chinese counterpart in Beijing.

OPPOSITION

Ozgur Ozel, head of the main opposition Republican People's Party (CHP), will attend the court hearing of a district mayor from his party in Istanbul, visit Istanbul Mayor Ekrem Imamoglu and others at the Silivri prison, and later attend an event with farmers. (0700 GMT) (0800 GMT) (1100 GMT)

BANKS ASSOCIATION

The general council of the Turkish Banks Association (TBB) will be held in Istanbul. (0800 GMT)

FOREIGN ARRIVALS (TRTARR=ECI)

The tourism ministry will release foreign arrivals data for April (0800 GMT).

INFLATION EXPECTATIONS

Turkish central bank will announce the results of inflation expectations by sector for May. (0700 GMT)

CENTRAL BANK

Turkey's central bank left its year-end inflation forecast unchanged at 24% on Thursday but Governor Fatih Karahan said it is ready to tighten policy if inflation worsens, after having pivoted to raising interest rates last month.

TURKEY-SYRIA

Turkey will provide 2 billion cubic metres of natural gas to Syria each year, Turkish energy minister Alparslan Bayraktar said on Thursday.

Turkey will start supplying Syria, which has suffered from severe power shortages, with gas in June as the two work on completing a gas pipeline connecting them, the Syrian energy minister told a press conference on Thursday.

KURDISH MILITANTS

Turkish President Tayyip Erdogan said Syria's government must keep focused on its deal with the Kurdish, U.S.-backed Syrian Democratic Forces (SDF) under which it is to integrate into the Syrian armed forces, pressing Damascus for its implementation.

Syrian Kurdish parties will send a delegation to Damascus soon for talks over their region's political future, a leading Kurdish politician said, as they seek to advance their goal of regional autonomy despite opposition from the Islamist rulers.

GREECE-TURKEY

Turkey must lift a 30-year old war threat against Greece if it wants Athens to consent to Ankara accessing European Union defence funds, Greek Prime Minister Kyriakos Mitsotakis said on Thursday.

CELEBI DISPUTE WITH INDIA

India's government told a court on Thursday it was well within New Delhi's legal powers to revoke Turkey-based Celebi's clearance to provide aviation ground services without giving it advance warning, given the matter concerned national security.

data.title: Aston Minerals, Torque Metals Say Supreme Court to Hear Application to Approve Proposed Merger
data.description:
Aston Minerals and Torque Metals said the Supreme Court of Western Australia will hear an application on May 28 to approve the proposed merger of the companies via schemes of arrangement, according to a Thursday filing with the Australian bourse.

Shares of Aston Minerals fell nearly 5% in recent Friday trade, while Torque Metals' shares added 4%.

data.title: Belgrade bourse blue-chip index inches up
data.description:
BELGRADE (Serbia), May 23 (SeeNews) - The blue-chip index of the Belgrade Stock Exchange, BELEX15, added 0.10% on Thursday, closing at 1,159.93 points, bourse data showed.

Industrial gases producer Messer Tehnogas [BEL:TGAS], which generated the largest single turnover of 2.9 million dinars for the day, advanced by 1.11% to close at 34,727 dinars. Insurer Dunav Osiguranje [BEL:DNOS] rose by 0.29% to 1,394 dinars at close in a trade of 469 shares.

At the other end, Aerodrom Nikola Tesla [BEL:AERO] declined by 0.64%, closing at 2,186 dinars as 738 shares changed hands, while cookware maker Metalac [BEL:MTLC] shed 0.05% to close at 1,950 dinars as 76 shares traded.

No other blue-chip companies traded on Thursday.

The broader BELEXline, a free-float market capitalisation-weighted index, added 0.06% to close at 2,649.54 points.

The total turnover on the Belgrade bourse rose to 11 million dinars ($105,988/ 93,812 euro) from 4.2 million dinars on Wednesday. Trading in government securities contributed some 4.5 million dinars to the total on Thursday, compared to 921,045 dinars on Wednesday.

(1 euro = 117.260 dinars)

data.title: China's yuan inches higher, set for fifth straight weekly gain
data.description:
China's yuan inched higher against the dollar and looked set for the fifth straight weekly gain on Friday, thanks largely to a weakening greenback in overseas markets on investor concerns over U.S. fiscal challenges.

Broad dollar weakness extended after Moody's last week downgraded its U.S. debt ratings, turning investor attention to Washington's $36 trillion debt pile and U.S. President Donald Trump's tax bill that could add trillions more to it.

The weaker dollar and rising U.S. yields have pushed out the yield gap between China's ultra-long 30-year government bonds (CN30YT=RR), (CN230023=CFXS) and their U.S. counterparts (US30YT=RR) to the widest on record.

The yuan, along with other non-dollar currencies, gained some support and has risen about 1% in both onshore and offshore markets this month.

As of 0356 GMT, the onshore yuan was 0.06% higher at 7.2015 per dollar, while its offshore counterpart was up about 0.08% in Asian trade to 7.1994.

If the onshore yuan maintains its current level, it would have gained 0.12% to the dollar for the week to book the fifth consecutive weekly rise.

However, gains were limited as buying interest in cheaper greenback rose around the psychologically important 7.2 per dollar level from both corporate clients and major state-owned banks, multiple traders said.

Some overseas-listed Chinese companies usually have higher foreign exchange demand between May and August to make dividend payments to their offshore shareholders. Meanwhile, state bank dollar buying was widely interpreted as an attempt to slow the yuan's gains.

Some market participants believe a too-strong Chinese currency could hurt the vast export sector at a time of huge uncertainty around Sino-U.S. trade relations still persisted.

"While we remain cautious about the prospects of a comprehensive China-U.S. trade agreement following the 90-day truce, we are increasingly confident in the Chinese economy's resilience amid ongoing trade uncertainty, particularly as both sides have ruled out a full-scale decoupling," said Serena Zhou, senior China economist at Mizuho Securities.

That refers to the 90-day tariff truce agreed by Washington and Beijing earlier this month to pause their trade war and roll back a majority of the duties levied on each other's goods since April.

Prior to the market opening, the People's Bank of China (PBOC) set the midpoint rate , around which the yuan is allowed to trade in a 2% band, at 7.1919 per dollar and 232 pips firmer than a Reuters' estimate of 7.2151.

Zhu Haibin, chief China economist at J.P. Morgan, said yuan's depreciation pressure has eased as tariff risks fell.

"How much the yuan will go next actually depends largely on China's exporters," Zhu said.

China's broad foreign exchange reserves did not rise much in 2024, when the trade surplus grew close to $1 trillion. It "means that many exporters chose to keep the dollars. In the case of a weaker U.S. dollar, how much positions will be adjusted will have a relatively large impact on the strength of the yuan," he said.

J.P. Morgan expects the yuan to swing between 7.2 and 7.3 per dollar in the next six to 12 months.

data.title: Bond markets in the driver's seat
data.description:
A look at the day ahead in European and global markets from Ankur Banerjee

After a volatile week when markets zeroed in on major economies' precarious fiscal health, with a sell-off in Treasuries and government bonds from Japan and Britain, fixed income investors are now setting the broader market agenda.

Lacklustre bond auctions in Japan and the U.S. this week underscored the lack of demand for long tenors, as investors send a clear message to governments that in the current climate of uncertainty they need to pay more to borrow for decades down the road.

While beaten-down Treasuries found buyers by the end of the week, The 30-year bond (US30YT=RR) yield remained above the psychologically important 5% threshold in Asian hours on Friday.

Super-long Japanese government bonds were stable but still near record highs touched earlier this week. The 10-year gilt yield (GB10YT=RR) hit its highest since January 15 on Thursday.

Wherever you look, there are signs of unease in the markets about the stretched finances of the biggest economies.

Fiscal worries have put pressure on the dollar, which is on course for a weekly loss against the euro and the yen for the first time in five weeks. Safe-haven flows are back, too, with gold set to clock its best week in more than a month.

Since Moody's downgraded its U.S. debt ratings last week, investor attention has fixated on the country's $36 trillion debt pile and U.S. President Donald Trump's tax bill, which could add trillions of dollars more.

Dubbed a "big, beautiful bill" by Trump, it narrowly passed the Republican-controlled U.S. House of Representatives and now heads to the Senate for what is likely to be weeks of debate, keeping investors on the edge.

Investors are also pining for more good news on trade deals as we hit the halfway point of the 90-day pause to Trump's so-called reciprocal tariffs.

Key developments that could influence markets on Friday:

Economic events: Germany GDP data for Q1, UK retail sales data for April

Trying to keep up with the latest tariff news?

Our new daily news digest offers a rundown of the top market-moving headlines impacting global trade. Sign up for Tariff Watch here.

data.title: Lenovo's PC Business Likely to See Diminished Tariff Impact in 2H — Market Talk
data.description: The impact from tariffs on Lenovo's PC business could diminish in 2H, DBS analyst Jim Au says in a research note. Lenovo's China+1 strategy for PC manufacturing should help it mitigate future hits from U.S. tariffs, the analyst says. While macroeconomic headwinds may continue to dampen consumer sentiment toward PCs, the AI-driven replacement cycle could moderate overall impacts, he notes. DBS expects a surge in Lenovo's AI PC shipments by over 90% in fiscal 2026. Meanwhile, a recovery in its hardware segment's operating margin should begin in the September quarter as Lenovo ramps up its production in Vietnam. DBS maintains a buy call on Lenovo as its sustainable market share gains positions the company well within the AI adoption megatrend, he adds. DBS's target price for Lenovo is currently under review. Shares are last flat at HK$9.57. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

data.title: apan 10-Year Yield Hovers Near 2008 Highs
data.description:
Japan’s 10-year government bond yield held steady around 1.56% on Friday, lingering near its highest level since 2008, as stronger-than-expected inflation data fueled expectations of further policy tightening by the Bank of Japan.

Core inflation rose to 3.5% year-on-year in April—surpassing forecasts and marking the highest reading in over two years—while headline inflation remained unchanged at 3.6%.

The data reinforced the view that inflationary pressures are becoming more entrenched, prompting markets to price in a higher terminal policy rate.

BOJ board member Asahi Noguchi acknowledged that the recent rise in Japanese yields reflects shifting market expectations about future rate levels.

However, he emphasized that there is currently no need to significantly alter the central bank’s bond tapering plan.

The BOJ is gradually reducing its bond purchases by ¥400 billion per quarter, targeting a monthly purchase pace of approximately ¥2.9 trillion by spring 2026.

data.title: Mulkia Investment Buys Saudi Property for SAR33 Million
data.description:

The land spans 11,138.11 square meters and is located in the Al-Faisaliah neighborhood, according to a Thursday filing. Mulkia Investment is the fund manager and operator of Mulkia Gulf Real Estate REIT Fund.

The lender said the number of treasury shares stood at 16,191,561 shares following the transaction.

data.title: Australia's ALS down ahead of annual results; brokerages cautious
data.description:
** Shares of Australia's ALS Limited down nearly 1% at A$17.565

** Mineral testing services provider to report FY25 results on May 27

** Jefferies maintains "buy" rating at PT A$18.80

** Brokerage says ALQ's biggest segment, minerals, has become a positive driver after being a drag on the company for most of FY24 and FY25

** Expects signs for medium-term growth in results

** However, Jarden downgrades ALS to "underweight" from "neutral"

** Brokerage anticipates some positive commentary on co's commodities division over strengthening volume growth, but believes its share price reflects potential upsides

** It marginally hikes PT to A$15 from A$14.5 to reflect expected modest EPS uplift by nearly 1% for FY26 and FY27

** Stock up 16.6% YTD, including current session's moves

data.title: Malaysia Leading Index Fall for 3rd Month
data.description:
Malaysia’s leading economic index edged down 0.04% month-on-month in March 2025, following a revised 0.02% decline in the previous month, marking its third drop this year.

Four out of seven components declined, including the Bursa Malaysia Industrial Index (-0.2% vs -0.1% in February), real imports of semiconductors (-0.2% vs 0.3%), approved housing units (-0.1% vs -0.05%), and new company registrations (-0.1% vs -0.1%).

On the upside, real imports of basic precious and non-ferrous metals rose (0.3% vs 0.1%), expected manufacturing sales value rebounded (0.2% vs -0.2%), and real money supply increased (0.1% vs -0.03%).

On a yearly basis, the index grew by 0.6%, accelerating from a 0.1% rise in February and marking the strongest pace in three months.

Meanwhile, the coincident index, which reflects current economic conditions, declined by 0.2%, swinging a marginally revised 1.8% gain in February.
